title: Bat Removal & Exclusion in Crown Heights, Brooklyn | $900 – $2,500
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in Crown Heights, Brooklyn. Safe, humane bat control for brownstones and multi-family buildings. Licensed experts. Call today!
intro: Crown Heights residents dealing with bats in attics, chimneys, or building voids need fast, professional bat removal and exclusion services. Our licensed wildlife control experts provide safe, humane bat extraction and permanent exclusion solutions tailored to Crown Heights' unique mix of historic brownstones, multi-family homes, and apartment buildings. We ensure your property is bat-free while protecting these important animals according to NYC regulations.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC specializes in bat removal throughout Crown Heights, with extensive experience working in the neighborhood's classic Brooklyn architecture and multi-unit dwellings. Our certified technicians understand local building codes, use humane exclusion methods, and provide comprehensive sealing services to prevent re-entry, all backed by our satisfaction guarantee.
localContext: Crown Heights' beautiful pre-war brownstones, historic row houses along Eastern Parkway, and older apartment buildings near Prospect Park often feature the architectural gaps, chimneys, and attic spaces that attract roosting bats. The tree-lined streets and proximity to green spaces like the Brooklyn Botanic Garden make this neighborhood particularly attractive to bat populations seeking urban shelter.

faqs.question: How much does bat removal cost in Crown Heights?
faqs.answer: Bat removal and exclusion services in Crown Heights typically range from $900 to $2,500, depending on the size of the colony, number of entry points, and the complexity of your building's structure. Brownstones and multi-story buildings may require additional work. We provide free inspections and detailed estimates before beginning any work.

faqs.question: Is bat removal legal in Crown Heights, Brooklyn?
faqs.answer: Yes, but it must be done humanely and according to NYC and New York State regulations. Bats are protected species, so removal must be conducted by licensed professionals using exclusion methods that allow bats to leave but not return. Timing is also important—bat removal cannot be performed during maternity season (roughly May through August) when flightless young are present.

faqs.question: How do I know if I have bats in my Crown Heights home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include seeing bats enter or exit your building at dusk, hearing scratching or squeaking sounds in attics or walls, finding small dark droppings (guano) near entry points, or noticing a strong ammonia-like odor. Crown Heights' older buildings often have bats in chimneys, attic spaces, or behind exterior siding and trim.

faqs.question: What does the bat exclusion process involve?
faqs.answer: Our process begins with a thorough inspection to locate all entry points and assess the colony size. We then install one-way exclusion devices that allow bats to exit but not return, seal all secondary entry points, and clean affected areas. After all bats have left (typically 3-7 days), we remove the devices and permanently seal all openings. We also provide attic restoration and sanitation services if needed.
